close_reason: README and docs folder now cover the current delegated worker lifecycle, landing policies, reviewer config, command/tool surface, and quickstart/operator workflows.
description:
Turn the existing docs/tests sink into the full documentation pass needed before merging and dogfooding.

Scope:
- refresh the package README so it serves as the overview + quickstart for the beadwork extension
- document the real operator workflow for `/bw engage`, `/bw delegate`, `/bw run`, `/bw workers`, and `/bw land`
- add/update config examples covering worktree bootstrap, worker model overrides, landing policies (`auto` vs `deferred`), reviewer-agent configuration, remediation/validation behavior, and relevant env vars
- move deeper/reference material into a `docs/` folder so README stays oriented around overview, setup, common workflows, and example configs
- make sure recent behavior changes are covered truthfully: background supervision cadence, worker logs/notifications, deferred landing states, review verdicts, reviewer artifact budget naming, and 30-minute reviewer timeout defaults
- add any needed tests/docs adjustments so the documented workflow matches the current implementation

Goal: after this ticket, the extension should have an accurate top-level README and supporting docs that codify the real current workflow well enough to merge to main and continue dogfooding.

comments.text: Landed docs pass in commit f2f5cc9. README now serves as overview/quickstart/config examples, with deeper references in packages/pi-beadwork-extension/docs/{README,workflows,configuration,commands}. Also documented deferred landing, reviewer gating, supervisor cadence, /bw land, the reviewer maxArtifactChars rename, and the 30-minute reviewer timeout default.

title: Add comprehensive README/docs for delegated worker lifecycle and landing modes
